title: LLM decoder distribution robustness sweep
hypothesis: Decoder approximation quality depends strongly on prompt, logit-margin, entropy, and probability dynamic-range distributions, so the next architecture step should broaden the prompt regimes and record distribution rollups before tuning any single approximation parameter.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Across a broader rough prompt-regime set, which integer and fp-like decoder probability-path approximations preserve next-token and top-k rank as entropy and top-1/top-2 margins vary?
include: 12 prompt-regime samples covering factual, arithmetic, phrase, list, code-like, dialogue, punctuation, long-context, repetition, and ambiguous-short prompts
reference entropy, effective vocabulary size, top-k probability mass, and top-1/top-2 logit margin rollups
candidate entropy, score-sum, effective vocabulary size, and top-1/top-2 score margin rollups
fp16, bf16, fp8_e5m2, and fp8_e4m3 final probability probes
approximate PWL softmax probes with integer and bf16-like paths
exclude: selecting a final hardware probability format
follow_on_broad_sweep: add weight/model-family diversity after this prompt/logit-regime map
use the entropy/margin buckets to choose narrower approximation sweeps

expected_benefit: turns the previous fixed-prompt fp/q evidence into a broader rank robustness map
makes the distribution dependence visible in committed artifacts
reduces the risk of overfitting approximation choices to math_two_plus_two or any single prompt
risks: the broader prompt set still uses the same tiny ONNX model and tokenizer
software-emulated quantization remains an approximation of future hardware
prompt-regime labels are coarse until a larger dataset is added
